instruction: Reply to Ana Morales at Ridgeview Clinic about the Spanish discharge instructions wording check. Tell her I can revise the medication paragraph, ask her to attach the latest English source version so I match the current packet, and mention that I'll keep the wording clear and plain for patients.

rule_ref.rule_text: For messages that represent her professionally, Esther prefers a draft-first workflow instead of sending immediately.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: I always want professional messages drafted first so I can review the wording before anything goes out.

attributes.subject: Spanish discharge instructions wording check
attributes.body: Hi Esther, we're updating the discharge packet for an upcoming patient rollout and need a quick review of the Spanish instructions. Could you send back a revised paragraph for the medication section and let me know if there are any wording changes you recommend for clarity?

attributes.subject: Lunch after errands?
attributes.body: Hey Esther, are you around this afternoon and want to grab lunch near the market? No rush if you're busy.

attributes.subject: April subscription invoice available
attributes.body: Your monthly MedScribe Portal invoice is ready to view in the billing center.
